name: Frankfurt Crown Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat egg yolks separately until light and creamy.
2. Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy, about 5 minutes.
3. Add egg yolks one at a time to the butter mixture.
4. Stir in lemon rind and rum.
5. Sift together baking powder and flour; gently fold into the batter.
6. Beat egg whites until stiff but not dry.
7. Gently fold egg whites into the batter.
8. Grease a 10-inch tube pan and pour in batter.
9. Bake at 325°F for about 60 minutes or until done.
10. Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto wire rack to cool completely.
11. Slice cake crosswise into three layers.
12. Pour rum over each layer.
For Buttercream Filling:
1. In a saucepan, combine sugar and water; cook to soft ball stage (238°F).
2. Beat egg yolks until light; gradually add hot syrup while beating.
3. Add rum and beat in butter until smooth and spreadable.
Praline Topping:
1. Melt butter and boil sugar with water to 310°F.
2. Cook almonds until caramelized, then grind into powder.
3. Strain apricot jam for glaze.
Assembly:
1. Place bottom cake layer on plate; spread with half the buttercream.
2. Repeat with second layer.
3. Top with third layer and spread remaining buttercream.
4. Drizzle with apricot glaze and sprinkle praline powder.
